title: sinoga lysicephala
intro: Sinoga lysicephala, also known as Sida cordifolia, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating symptoms of dysentery and diarrhea. In herbal preparations, Sida cordifolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
